response.justification: Excellent technical engineer with 19+ years of software development and demonstrated ability to mentor others, but lacks the critical people management experience required for this manager role. The position requires 2 years of direct people management or team leadership experience—the resume shows no evidence of managing direct reports, conducting performance reviews, or leading teams. While the candidate has touched developer tools and infrastructure (GitLab, CI/CD, MCP frameworks), this work is secondary to their primary focus on product engineering, not the 3-year developer tools core competency needed. Strong technical foundation could enable transition into management with significant mentoring, but they don't currently meet minimum requirements.
strengths: 19+ years of software development experience spanning web, mobile, desktop, embedded systems with hands-on architectural contributions
Proven ability to mentor and transfer knowledge through workshops, training courses, code katas, and open-source community engagement
Hands-on technical depth with demonstrated expertise in infrastructure, tooling, and complex system design (CI/CD, distributed systems, testing frameworks)
concerns: Zero formal people management or direct report experience despite senior titles; no evidence of performance reviews, team development, or organizational leadership
Developer tools experience is scattered and secondary focus; primary career has been product engineering, not building tools as core responsibility
Pattern of 3-4 year tenures across 12+ employers suggests potential difficulties with long-term organizational commitment or strategic alignment
